Executive summary: Iteration 000024 substantially implements the PRD: the backend now loads ACEStep from separate diffusion, text encoder, and VAE files; the legacy checkpoint path is removed; configuration validation happens before inference; pipeline caching remains in place; and targeted backend audit tests passed under the project's uv environment. Full compliance is blocked by the PRD's lint/typecheck acceptance criteria because repository-wide typecheck passes but repository-wide lint currently fails.
Minor observations: Targeted backend audit tests passed with `uv run --project backend python -m pytest backend/test_audio_repository.py backend/test_main.py -q` (34 passed).
Repository-wide typecheck passed with `npm run typecheck`.
Repository-wide lint failed with 116 errors, primarily in frontend React Three Fiber components and hook/ref purity rules, so the PRD's lint/typecheck acceptance criteria are not fully satisfied.
Backend startup/shutdown still uses FastAPI `on_event`, which emits deprecation warnings during tests.
Pydantic emits `UnsupportedFieldAttributeWarning` warnings for some aliased fields during backend tests.
Conclusions and recommendations: Functional requirements FR-1 through FR-4 comply. User stories are only partially compliant because the iteration's implementation-level acceptance criteria are met but the stated lint/typecheck gate is not fully green. Recommended next step: either refactor the codebase until `npm run lint` passes, or explicitly narrow the PRD quality gate to the backend/touched scope if full repo lint was not intended for this iteration. Secondary cleanup recommendations are to migrate FastAPI startup/shutdown handlers to lifespan and review the Pydantic alias warnings.
